question: When using a C++ vector, which of the following operations has a time complexity of $O(n)$ due to the need to shift elements?

answers.feedback: Incorrect. Adding to the end is O(1) amortized because no elements need to be shifted.
Incorrect. Removing the last element is O(1) as no shifting is required.
Incorrect. Changing a value at a specific index is a direct access operation, which is O(1).
Correct! Inserting at the front requires every existing element in the vector to be shifted one position to the right, which takes linear time.
